summary: Tested the new per-State-Machine actions[] feature (sibling to states[]/transitions[]). 11 backend pytest cases + frontend Playwright smoke all pass. Confirmed: catalog endpoint returns 16 items; GET list/single auto-migrates legacy SMs (Legacy Lifecycle had its actions[] auto-derived from transitions); POST/PUT validate dup keys, invalid kind, and transitions referencing undefined actions; POST auto-derives actions[] from transitions when empty; PUT rename works when transitions are renamed in same payload and is rejected when transitions still reference old key. Default Marketing Request Lifecycle has exactly 9 actions and 12 transitions, and the existing end-to-end MR lifecycle regression still passes. Frontend: editor renders 9 action rows with key/label/description/kind editors, transition action dropdown is scoped to sm.actions only (not the 16-item global catalog), 'Add action' appends a row, and deleting the 'close' action (used by 1 transition) is correctly blocked with a toast error.

issues: The blocked-delete toast fires twice for a single click (saw two identical 'Cannot delete Mark Completed — used by 1 transition(s)' toasts). Likely double-invocation of the toast call inside removeAction. Cosmetic only — behavior is correct.

action_items: (Minor / optional) De-duplicate the toast call in StateMachines.js removeAction() — it currently surfaces twice when delete is blocked.
critical_code_review_comments: _migrate_actions_inplace is idempotent and side-effect-free for already-migrated docs — good. Returns the same doc reference; callers don't have to re-fetch.
POST endpoint's auto-derivation correctly re-runs validation against the derived actions list (so dup/invalid kind paths still fire even with empty payload).
PUT only re-validates when states/actions/transitions are present in the partial payload — this is correct but means a PUT that only changes 'name' will skip validation. Acceptable.
Action.kind defaults to 'neutral' on the Pydantic model, so a payload like {key:'x', label:'X'} (no kind) passes validation. Confirmed working.
Frontend transition dropdown shows '— define an action first —' placeholder when sm.actions is empty (per spec).
Legacy SM auto-migration uses ACTION_KIND_HINTS — verified for the inserted 'Legacy Lifecycle (no actions)' record which received kind=positive for close and kind=neutral for reopen.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_sm_<uuid> records and delete them on teardown. No persistent test data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Per-SM actions[] feature is fully in place. Backend test file is /app/backend/tests/test_sm_actions.py — keep it as the regression for this feature. Default MR SM = 9 actions / 12 transitions; do NOT delete the 'Legacy Lifecycle (no actions)' record (used to validate auto-migration path).
